name: Riera de Rubió and Jorba Lock Loop
name_original: Riera de Rubió y Resclosa de Jorba — circular desde Jorba
description: This difficult 8.3-mile (13.3 km) jogging route from Jorba offers varied terrain, panoramic views, and birdwatching opportunities.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Jorba offer diverse terrain and scenic natural features in Catalonia, Spain. The region is characterized by varied landscapes, including the Riera de Rubió stream, the Anoia River, and the prominent Puig de Sant Miquel hill. Joggers can find paths that traverse both natural areas and historical points of interest, providing a range of experiences. Best jogging routes around Jorba The most popular jogging route is Riera de Rubió and Jorba Lock Loop, an 8.3 miles (13.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 40 minutes to complete. This route features diverse landscapes and opportunities for birdwatching. Another top favourite among local runners is Jorba Lock – Puig de Sant Miquel loop from Jorba, a difficult 10.0 miles (16.1 km) path. This trail offers challenging terrain and views towards Puig de Sant Miquel. Local runners also love the Jorba Lock – Jorba Dam loop from Jorba, a 6.2 miles (10.0 km) trail leading through the area around the Jorba Lock and Dam, often completed in about 1 hour 10 minutes. Jogging around Jorba is defined by diverse terrain, the Riera de Rubió, and the Anoia River, with routes ranging from moderate to difficult. The network offers options for different ability levels, including paths suitable for good fitness and sure-footedness. The routes in Jorba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1,400 runners have used komoot to explore Jorba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Jorba?
A: Jorba offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 150 trails to explore. These include options for various fitness levels, with 11 easy routes, over 100 moderate trails, and more than 40 challenging paths for experienced runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Jorba's running trails?
A: The running trails around Jorba feature diverse terrain, ranging from paths along the &lt;em&gt;Riera de Rubió&lt;/em&gt; and &lt;em&gt;Anoia River&lt;/em&gt; to more challenging ascents towards &lt;em&gt;Puig de Sant Miquel&lt;/em&gt;. You'll encounter varied landscapes, including natural areas and historical points of interest, with both paved and accessible sections, as well as more rugged trails requiring good fitness.

Q: Are there any running routes with significant elevation gain in Jorba?
A: Yes, Jorba offers several routes with notable elevation gain. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5332591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorba Lock – Puig de Sant Miquel loop from Jorba&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.0-mile (16.1 km) path with an elevation gain of approximately 377 meters, providing a good challenge for runners seeking ascents.

Q: Can I find moderate running trails in Jorba?
A: Absolutely. Many of Jorba's running routes are classified as moderate, offering a balanced challenge. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31509265" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorba Lock – Jorba Dam loop from Jorba&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.2-mile (10.0 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 10 minutes to complete, leading through scenic areas around the lock and dam.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options around Jorba?
A: For those looking for longer runs, Jorba has several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10475450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorba Lock – Puig de Sant Miquel loop from el Pla de Rubió&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 18.0 km (11.2 miles) route with over 500 meters of elevation gain, ideal for endurance training.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the running routes?
A: Jorba's running routes often feature beautiful natural elements and historical landmarks. You can explore the picturesque &lt;em&gt;Riera de Rubió&lt;/em&gt;, the &lt;em&gt;Anoia River&lt;/em&gt; with its pedestrian path, and the scenic &lt;em&gt;Jorba Lock and Dam&lt;/em&gt;. Some trails also lead towards the prominent &lt;em&gt;Puig de Sant Miquel&lt;/em&gt;. Additionally, you might encounter an 11th-century Romanesque hermitage, adding historical charm to your run.

Q: Are there any waterfalls along the running trails in Jorba?
A: Yes, the region around Jorba features a small waterfall along the &lt;em&gt;Riera de Rubió&lt;/em&gt;. This natural stream is a key feature of several jogging trails and collects water from the nearby mountain slopes, offering a refreshing sight during your run.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Jorba?
A: The running routes in Jorba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.8 stars from over 40 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse landscapes, the variety of terrain, and the opportunities for birdwatching, particularly along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113739480/riera-de-rubio-and-jorba-lock-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Rubió and Jorba Lock Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Jorba?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Jorba are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1113739480/riera-de-rubio-and-jorba-lock-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Rubió and Jorba Lock Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24439316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorba loop from Jorba&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 9.4 km (5.8 miles) trail.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or peaks accessible via running trails?
A: For rewarding views, consider routes that ascend to higher points. The &lt;em&gt;Puig de Sant Miquel&lt;/em&gt;, a mountain exceeding seven hundred meters in the Serra de Rubió range, offers challenging terrain and potentially panoramic vistas. You can find routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5332591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jorba Lock – Puig de Sant Miquel loop from Jorba&lt;/a&gt; that incorporate this peak. Other viewpoints in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5694171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Planes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites to see while running in Jorba?
A: Yes, Jorba's landscape is dotted with historical interest. You might encounter an 11th-century Romanesque hermitage with a beautiful arched doorway, which adds a touch of historical charm to the natural surroundings. Additionally, the area features several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5699836" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tossa and Montbui Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3270107" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Claramunt Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which can be seen from some trails or visited nearby.
tips: Resclosa that has the same name as the town and where water is found in abundance throughout the year. Nice area to take a break!
Small waterfall that is located in the same Rubió river and that collects the waters of the slopes of the nearby mountains.

The Anoia River passes through the town of Jorba. There is a pedestrian path that follows the bank of the same river and that lets us see the town of Jorba and the waters of one of the most famous rivers in the area.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog this circular route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this route usually takes around 1 hour and 40 minutes to 2 hours to complete, depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging trail?
faqs.answer: While some might consider it an easy route for general excursions, it's categorized as difficult for jogging. This is due to its varied terrain, which includes ascents to viewpoints like Sant Salvador, and a mix of paths, tracks, and unpaved sections.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain will I encounter on this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied experience, encompassing paths, tracks, and agroforestry roads. You'll jog through unpaved sections, areas with stone walls and vineyards, forest trails, and cart paths. There's also a wetland area characterized by poplars and reeds.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience beautiful scenery throughout. Key highlights include the Jorba Lock and the Rubió Stream Waterfall. The climb to the Sant Salvador peak (574 meters) offers expansive views of the Conca d'Òdena, including Igualada, Montserrat, and the coastal mountain ranges. The Resclosa de Jorba also forms a small pond, perfect for birdwatching.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn often offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be warm, so consider early morning or late afternoon jogs. Winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas, near wildlife, or when encountering other trail users.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Jorba, you can typically find parking within the town of Jorba. Look for designated parking areas or suitable street parking close to the trail's starting point.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access or jog this trail. It is open to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that overlap with the E11, Carril bici N-II (Sant Genís - Santa Maria del Camí), Camí Ignasià Català 5: Cervera - Igualada, Camí de Sant Jaume (l'Hospitalet - Tàrrega), and the GR 7.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a jogging trip on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and 'difficult' rating for jogging, we recommend bringing appropriate trail running shoes, sufficient water, and snacks. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also advisable. Depending on the season, consider layers of clothing and sun protection.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its classification as 'difficult' for jogging and the varied terrain including ascents, this trail might be challenging for absolute beginners. Those new to trail jogging might prefer to build up their fitness on easier routes before tackling this one.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead in Jorba?
faqs.answer: Jorba is a small town, so you may find basic amenities such as cafes or small shops. It's always a good idea to check local opening hours, especially if you plan to rely on them for refreshments before or after your jog.
